summary.intent: Fix two P2 findings (0CV, 0CY) from a Codex review pass on a Rust file upload component: make ReconcileState drag-aware and prevent RetryFile from incorrectly starting unrelated pending files.
summary.outcome: Both fixes implemented with TDD (148 tests passing, 0 uncovered branches), spec synced, and changes committed and pushed. Codex finding-6 was also confirmed resolved.

repo: File upload component lives in crates/ars-components/src/specialized/file_upload/mod.rs with a companion tests.rs
Spec is maintained in sync with code at spec/components/specialized/file-upload.md and validated via a spec validate command
Coverage target for the file_upload module is 100% (0 uncovered branches); crate-wide target appears to be ~90%
Codex review runs in iterative passes; findings can be resolved by pushback without code changes if the reviewer accepts the argument

code.finding: The will_retry scan's id-mismatch branch (inside a .any() iterator) was an uncovered branch; needed a test retrying a later file so the first file is evaluated as a mismatch before the match is found
ReconcileState (triggered when a controlled upload finishes mid-drag) must be drag-aware and return a drag-stranded target state, same pattern as the ku9 fix
RetryFile should chain StartUpload only when the retried file_id actually matches a failed file; previously it could fire StartUpload on unrelated pending files

workflow: Coverage must be checked after every fix cycle — new branches from logic changes can drop coverage even when all tests pass
Spec sync is part of the definition of done for each fix, not an afterthought
Codex findings are triaged per pass: clear mechanical fixes (0CV, 0CY) are implemented immediately; foundational design-fork findings (0Ca) are surfaced to the user rather than fixed unilaterally

friction: Coverage gap discovered after tests passed — required an additional investigation step to identify the uncovered branch (line 1083) and write a targeted test
open_items: Finding 0Ca (hgY optimistic sync_controlled means a controlled parent cannot veto a change — re-rendering with old prop produces no SetFiles) is a foundational pure-controlled-vs-optimistic fork that was intentionally deferred and surfaced to the user rather than fixed
